title: Bootstrap benchmark crate and module skeleton
description:
Context:
benchmark currently has only docs. Create the initial Rust crate and file layout that matches the module skeleton in docs/PLAN_BENCHMARK.md so later agents work against stable paths instead of inventing structure. Keep main thin so parallel feature work can happen in library modules instead of colliding in the entrypoint.

Deliverables:
- Cargo.toml with edition, release profile, and pinned core dependencies
- src/lib.rs as the module root plus module files for cli, assertions, candidate, key_check, compare, engine, lock_check, report, render, and refusal
- src/main.rs kept as a thin binary entrypoint shell
- a compileable shell with placeholder module wiring and zero benchmark semantics implemented yet
- minimal tests directory presence only; named suites and fixture corpus belong to bd-1ko

Suggested swarm lane:
foundation / repo bootstrap
acceptance_criteria:
1. cargo test compiles the crate shell successfully.
2. src/lib.rs owns module declarations and src/main.rs stays thin.
3. Every planned module path exists in the repo.
4. This issue does not absorb fixture-corpus or scoring behavior work that belongs to later tasks.
notes:
Target files: Cargo.toml, src/lib.rs, src/main.rs.
Reservation guidance: foundation owns module creation only; later tasks should not re-open Cargo.toml unless dependency changes are explicitly required.
close_reason: Scaffolded the benchmark crate, module file map, thin main.rs entrypoint, and compileable refusal shell.

title: Run doctor-mode audit and add read-only benchmark doctor
description: Run world-class-doctor-mode-for-cli-tools against benchmark in audit-first mode, then implement the first safe read-only doctor surface. Expected surface: benchmark doctor health, benchmark doctor capabilities --json, benchmark doctor robot-docs, and benchmark doctor --robot-triage or equivalent. Scope first slice to detection, scoring, robot docs, and tests. Do not add fix mode until the benchmark-specific failure-mode inventory has detector, backup, inverse, and fixture coverage. Acceptance: skill workspace artifacts or databook attached, repo-specific failure modes captured as follow-up beads, quality gate green, ubs on changed files, and br sync --flush-only.

title: Implement candidate loading for row-oriented formats
description:
Context:
Benchmark v0 only accepts one row-oriented relation. Implement extension-based format detection and DuckDB loading for CSV, JSONL, Parquet, and row-oriented JSON without guessing document semantics.

Deliverables:
- extension-based candidate format detection
- DuckDB loading path for supported formats
- row-oriented shape checks for JSON inputs
- refusal path for unsupported or document-shaped inputs

Suggested swarm lane:
data access / DuckDB
acceptance_criteria:
1. CSV, JSONL, Parquet, and row-oriented JSON all materialize deterministically.
2. Nested or document-shaped JSON is rejected rather than flattened heuristically.
3. Candidate loading stays isolated from scoring math and compare semantics.
notes: Taking over final validation after SapphireDune handoff. Working file: src/candidate.rs plus Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock only if validation requires a small follow-up.
close_reason: Candidate loading validated after handoff: fixed parallel temp-dir collisions in candidate tests, cargo test/clippy clean, ubs clean of criticals.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: Shared-tree follow-up validation completed after candidate-lane build contention cleared: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and ubs . are green.

title: Reconcile benchmark README and AGENTS with implemented crate layout
description:
Context:
README.md and AGENTS.md were written before the Rust crate exists so they describe the intended file map and workflow. Once scaffold and integration work land, re-check both files against the actual crate layout, real commands, and any CI/release surfaces that exist by then.

Deliverables:
- align file tables with actual paths
- remove any stale planned-only wording that is no longer true
- add real install/build/test/release details once they exist

Acceptance:
1. README and AGENTS match the implemented repo layout.
2. No command in either file points to a non-existent path or workflow.
3. Any newly real CI/release/install surface is documented exactly once.
close_reason: README.md and AGENTS.md now reflect the implemented crate, valid local commands, and current quality-gate state; git diff --check and ubs . validated the docs pass.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: Reopened from stale in_progress state: no assignee, no comments, and no active file reservation. Picking it back up explicitly through docs reconciliation.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: Starting docs reconciliation lane. Reserving exact files: README.md and AGENTS.md. Verifying live crate layout, test surfaces, and current CLI behavior before patching docs.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: Docs reconciliation pass completed: re-read README.md and AGENTS.md against the live crate, refreshed AGENTS quick-reference/triage workflow with bv robot commands, updated stale repo-state wording, and verified cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and ubs . on the current tree. README already matched the implemented crate surface, so no further README edits were needed in this pass.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: Updated README.md and AGENTS.md to match the implemented crate, current CLI surface, quality-gate workflow, and actual file map. Validated with git diff --check and ubs . (0 critical findings).

title: Establish benchmark fixture corpus and named test suites
description:
Context:
Create the shared fixture and test harness surface early so the rest of the swarm can land work against stable paths, stable IDs, and stable naming conventions. This issue owns harness shape, not final feature completeness.

Deliverables:
- tests/fixtures layout for candidates, assertions, locks, and expected outputs
- named test files for cli, scoring_matrix, refusals, lock_integration, and perf_smoke or benches equivalent
- fixture naming conventions that map cleanly to BENCH_U and BENCH_I coverage
- at least one smoke fixture per supported candidate family, one malformed/refusal fixture family, and one representative larger perf fixture family or generator hook

Non-goals:
- implementing the actual comparison or scoring behavior
- closing the full test matrix by itself

Suggested swarm lane:
fixtures / test harness
acceptance_criteria:
1. The fixture tree and named test files exist and compile.
2. BENCH_U and BENCH_I IDs have a clear home in the harness naming scheme.
3. A performance-fixture path exists for later runtime smoke work.
4. Later agents can add cases without renaming or relocating the harness surface.
notes: Taking fixture/harness lane. Reserving exact new files: tests/fixtures/README.md, tests/fixtures/assertions/refusal/bench_u001_malformed.jsonl, tests/fixtures/assertions/smoke/bench_i001_gold.jsonl, tests/fixtures/candidates/refusal/bench_i004_nested.json, tests/fixtures/candidates/smoke/bench_i001_candidate.csv, tests/fixtures/candidates/smoke/bench_i001_candidate.json, tests/fixtures/candidates/smoke/bench_i001_candidate.jsonl, tests/fixtures/candidates/smoke/bench_i001_candidate.parquet, tests/fixtures/locks/smoke/bench_i010_candidate.lock.json, tests/fixtures/expected/README.md, tests/fixtures/perf/generate_perf_smoke.sh, tests/scoring_matrix.rs, tests/lock_integration.rs, tests/perf_smoke.rs. Not touching tests/cli.rs or tests/refusals.rs.
close_reason: Established the shared fixture corpus, added named scoring/lock/perf harness suites, checked in smoke/refusal fixtures for supported candidate families, added a deterministic perf generator hook, and validated with cargo test/clippy plus ubs .

title: Implement benchmark v0 scoring primitive
description:
Context:
benchmark now has an implementation-grade plan in docs/PLAN_BENCHMARK.md, but the repo still has no code. This epic tracks the end-to-end delivery of the benchmark.v0 scoring primitive as defined by that plan.

Scope:
- one candidate artifact materialized as one row-oriented relation
- one assertions JSONL file
- explicit compare_as semantics
- deterministic PASS/FAIL/SKIP report in human and JSON modes
- optional lock verification before scoring

Non-goals:
- document-shaped candidate evaluation
- automatic gold-set generation
- policy decisions owned by assess

Exit criteria:
- the crate exists and the D1-D9 stages in PLAN_BENCHMARK.md are satisfied
- named BENCH_U/BENCH_I tests and quality gates are green
- benchmark is ready for swarm implementation without further architectural discovery
close_reason: Benchmark v0 scoring primitive is implemented end-to-end: D1-D9 landed, named BENCH_U/BENCH_I coverage and quality gates are green, deterministic JSON is proven, perf smoke guardrails are in place, and docs match the implemented crate.

title: Add claim-slot benchmark fixture family for article extraction maps
description: The cmdrvl-cli claims-verifier POC currently has to flatten decoding canon entries into a CSV with slot_id/value_text just to use benchmark. Add a first-class fixture family and contract guidance for scoring canonical claim-slot maps or JSONL candidates directly, so article extraction benchmarks do not need the CSV bridge.

title: Suppress benign third-party runtime warnings from benchmark success paths
description:
Context:
Successful benchmark runs currently emit benign third-party stderr noise from native dependencies (for example Arrow/DuckDB CPU probing warnings like sysctlbyname failures on restricted macOS environments) even when scoring succeeds and the JSON/human report is correct. This weakens the operator experience, pollutes tournament logs, and makes the tool feel less trustworthy than the underlying logic actually is.

Why this belongs in benchmark:
- PLAN_BENCHMARK.md defines benchmark as a report tool with deterministic stdout artifacts and stderr reserved for process diagnostics.
- Success-path runs should feel clean and intentional; unrelated dependency warnings should not leak into normal operator output.
- This is a generic spine-tool ergonomics issue, not a BDC-specific workaround.

Deliverables:
- identify the exact source of the benign runtime warnings in benchmark success paths
- suppress, redirect, or neutralize those warnings for normal successful runs without hiding real benchmark diagnostics
- keep refusal and genuine error stderr behavior intact
- add a regression test or smoke check that proves clean success-path stderr in the affected environment
- update README/AGENTS or plan examples if needed so the stderr contract is explicit

Acceptance criteria:
1. A normal PASS or FAIL scoring run no longer emits unrelated native dependency warnings on stderr.
2. Genuine benchmark diagnostics and refusal messages still surface correctly.
3. The fix preserves the existing JSON/human report contract and does not introduce benchmark-side policy logic.
4. The clean-stderr behavior is covered by a repeatable test or smoke guardrail.
close_reason: Captured benign native stderr noise during CLI execution so PASS/FAIL scoring runs stay clean, preserved refusal/error behavior, documented the stderr contract, and added binary-level regression coverage plus full quality-gate validation.

title: Integrate benchmark end-to-end command path
description:
Context:
This issue is the composition step after the feature lanes land. It should wire the existing modules into one working benchmark command path without adding new architectural surface.

Deliverables:
- main orchestration from CLI through assertions, candidate loading, key validation, compare, scoring, optional lock check, and rendering
- end-to-end PASS / FAIL / REFUSAL execution over shared fixtures
- final handoff points between core modules made explicit in code, not hidden in main

Non-goals:
- inventing new v0 behavior
- quality-gate closure work that belongs in bd-36u

Suggested swarm lane:
integration / composition
acceptance_criteria:
1. benchmark runs one PASS fixture, one FAIL fixture, and one REFUSAL fixture end to end.
2. Integration wiring does not duplicate logic already owned by parser, loader, compare, engine, render, or lock modules.
3. Integration-only fixes stay within the existing v0 plan boundaries.
notes:
Target files: src/main.rs plus minimal integration-touch files and end-to-end tests.
Reservation guidance: this is composition work; prefer calling existing module APIs over editing their internals.
close_reason: Composed the real benchmark command path through assertions, optional lock verification, candidate loading, key validation, scoring, refusal mapping, and rendering; added end-to-end PASS/FAIL/REFUSAL CLI coverage; validated with cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and ubs .

title: Close benchmark quality gates and determinism proof
description:
Context:
Once the full command path works end to end, this final issue proves the implementation against the named quality gates in PLAN_BENCHMARK.md, including the runtime smoke guardrails. This is stop-ship verification, not architecture discovery.

Deliverables:
- complete BENCH_U and BENCH_I coverage against the final code paths
- determinism check showing repeated JSON runs are byte-identical for the same fixture bytes
- documented pass of fmt, clippy, cargo test, ubs, and the perf smoke path
- any remaining acceptance mismatches resolved without expanding v0 scope

Suggested swarm lane:
release-quality verification
acceptance_criteria:
1. Gate 1 through Gate 6 in PLAN_BENCHMARK.md are explicitly satisfied.
2. Repeated JSON runs over the same fixture bytes are byte-identical.
3. fmt, clippy, cargo test, and ubs all pass and are recorded in the issue notes or final handoff.
notes:
Target files: verification-oriented tests, bench harness references, and minimal issue-note updates.
Reservation guidance: prove the system; do not expand scope or redesign modules here.
close_reason: Named BENCH_U/BENCH_I coverage aligned to the plan; repeated JSON runs are byte-identical; cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, perf smoke, and ubs . all pass.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: Starting release-quality verification lane. Reserving exact file: tests/cli.rs for the determinism gate proof unless validation shows the proof already exists.

title: Wire benchmark CLI surface and refusal plumbing
description:
Context:
Implement the top-level command contract from PLAN_BENCHMARK.md: argument parsing, exit-code mapping, mode selection flags, and refusal-envelope wiring. This issue stops before real scoring or report formatting.

Deliverables:
- clap-based CLI parsing for candidate path, assertions path, key, lock flags, and json mode
- top-level exit code mapping for PASS / FAIL / REFUSAL
- refusal-envelope structs or helpers plus CLI-side rendering hook
- help and version output wired through main

Non-goals:
- implementing comparison or scoring semantics
- owning the final human report formatting from bd-egv

Suggested swarm lane:
operator surface / CLI
acceptance_criteria:
1. Help and version output match the plan closely.
2. A refusal path emits the documented envelope shape.
3. The CLI shell is exercisable without real scoring logic behind it.
notes: Released after SapphireDune reserved src/cli.rs, src/refusal.rs, src/main.rs, tests/cli.rs
close_reason: CLI/refusal shell validated: clap contract, PASS/FAIL/REFUSAL exit mapping surface, refusal rendering, help/version, cargo test, fmt, clippy, and ubs all verified.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Starting CLI/refusal lane. Reserving exact files: src/lib.rs, src/cli.rs, src/refusal.rs, tests/cli.rs. No broad directory reservation.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Completed CLI/refusal plumbing. Added explicit PASS/FAIL/REFUSAL exit mapping, improved clap help text, kept scaffold refusal shell wired through main, and added tests in tests/cli.rs covering help, version, and human/JSON refusal output. Validation: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, ubs .

title: Implement explicit comparison engine semantics
description:
Context:
The benchmark tool only works if comparison semantics are explicit and deterministic. Implement string, number, percent, and date comparisons exactly as the plan defines them.

Deliverables:
- comparison functions or traits for string, number, percent, and date
- absolute tolerance handling for number and percent only
- no implicit fallback from failed numeric parsing to string equality
- deterministic failure detail values suitable for the internal report model

Suggested swarm lane:
comparison semantics
acceptance_criteria:
1. number comparisons honor absolute tolerance exactly.
2. percent mode does not reinterpret ratio-form decimals.
3. date mode normalizes canonical date text only.
4. Invalid tolerance usage is rejected upstream, not papered over here.
notes:
Target files: src/compare.rs and comparison-focused tests.
Reservation guidance: comparison semantics should be implementable without touching parser or candidate loader internals.
close_reason: Explicit comparison semantics are implemented in src/compare.rs for string, number, percent, and date; full current-tree cargo test, clippy, and ubs validation are green.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Starting comparison semantics lane. Reserving exact file: src/compare.rs. Keeping tests inline in that module unless scope expands.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Implemented explicit comparison semantics in src/compare.rs for string, number, percent, and date, including no numeric fallback, percent '%' enforcement, canonical date normalization, and negative-tolerance rejection. Focused compare tests passed earlier; full current-worktree cargo revalidation is presently blocked behind concurrent DuckDB/candidate builds in the shared checkout.

title: Implement scoring engine, report model, and summary math
description:
Context:
This is the core evaluator. It should join assertions to candidate rows, produce PASS/FAIL/SKIP outcomes, and construct the internal benchmark report object that later rendering code formats for humans or JSON.

Deliverables:
- assertion-to-candidate join/projection evaluation path
- PASS / FAIL / SKIP_ENTITY / SKIP_FIELD outcome generation
- report-domain structs for summary, failures, skips, and input_verification placeholders
- summary math for total, passed, failed, skipped, resolved, accuracy, coverage, and by_severity
- stable ordering of failures and skips in assertion order

Non-goals:
- final CLI formatting and serialization details from bd-egv

Suggested swarm lane:
core scoring engine
acceptance_criteria:
1. Missingness stays separate from failure.
2. accuracy is null when resolved is zero.
3. The renderer can consume the internal report model without re-deriving summary math.
4. The candidate relation is loaded once per run and reused across assertion evaluation.
5. Repeated runs over the same inputs emit the same row ordering.
notes: Releasing immediately after SapphireDune reservation. No edits made to src/engine.rs, src/report.rs, or tests/scoring_matrix.rs.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic scoring engine, report model, summary math, and scoring_matrix coverage; validated with cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and ubs .

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: Yielding this lane to SapphireDune to avoid file collisions on src/engine.rs, src/report.rs, and tests/scoring_matrix.rs. No local edits landed in those files from this pass.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: Baseline before new engine/report edits: shared worktree currently passes cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and ubs ..

title: Add operator summary render modes without changing the benchmark report contract
description:
Context:
`benchmark` is a report tool with stable JSON and human output contracts, but in real operator workflows people still end up writing ad hoc TSV wrappers just to scan outcomes, scores, refusal codes, and derived quality signals across many runs. Add optional summary render surfaces that are derived from the existing report/refusal model so operators can inspect results quickly without changing the canonical JSON artifact.

Deliverables:
- add an optional compact summary render mode for score reports and refusal envelopes
- add an optional TSV summary render mode with stable columns suitable for shell pipelines
- include at least: tool, version, candidate label, outcome, accuracy, coverage, failed, skipped, quality_band, and refusal code when present
- ensure summary modes are pure renderings of the existing report/refusal model and never recompute scoring semantics
- update README, AGENTS, fixtures, and render-focused tests to cover the new surfaces

Non-goals:
- changing the existing default human output
- changing the `benchmark.v0` JSON report or refusal schema
- adding policy or routing logic beyond the already-derived `policy_signals`

Plan alignment:
- stays inside the plan's 'report tool' boundary and output/render sections
- preserves the invariants that human and JSON modes reflect the same underlying report data
- sharpens operator ergonomics without widening benchmark scope
acceptance_criteria:
1. `benchmark` can emit a compact summary view and a TSV summary view for both scoring reports and refusals without changing existing JSON output.
2. Summary values are derived from the existing report/refusal model only; no second scoring or policy engine is introduced.
3. README, AGENTS, and render-focused tests document and verify the new surfaces.
4. The canonical JSON contract and existing default human output remain backward compatible.
close_reason: Added compact summary and TSV render modes derived from the existing report/refusal model, updated docs, and covered the new surfaces with CLI and render tests.

title: Implement benchmark report rendering for JSON and human modes
description:
Context:
Benchmark is a report tool. This issue owns rendering the existing internal report model into the documented JSON contract and the compact default human summary.

Deliverables:
- JSON serialization that matches PLAN_BENCHMARK.md
- compact human output for PASS / FAIL / REFUSAL paths
- handling for present or absent input_verification data without changing report semantics
- stable field ordering and deterministic line formatting where practical

Non-goals:
- owning summary math or report-model construction from bd-6oe

Suggested swarm lane:
report surfaces
acceptance_criteria:
1. Human mode never invents counts or semantics that differ from JSON mode.
2. Row-level failures and skips are readable in operator mode.
3. Refusal rendering preserves the JSON contract as the system of record.
notes:
Target files: src/render.rs and output-format tests.
Reservation guidance: render existing report data only; do not re-derive counts or outcome semantics here.
close_reason: Render lane validated in shared tree; cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test all pass.

title: Implement assertion parsing and semantic validation
description:
Context:
Benchmark correctness starts with a trustworthy assertion contract. Implement JSONL parsing and semantic validation for entity, field, compare_as, tolerance, severity, and source.

Deliverables:
- assertion structs and serde layer
- line-by-line JSONL loader with stable ordering
- validation for compare_as defaults and legal values
- validation that tolerance is only accepted for number and percent modes
- refusal mapping for malformed or semantically invalid assertions

Suggested swarm lane:
input contract / assertions
acceptance_criteria:
1. Malformed lines identify their source line.
2. Illegal compare_as or tolerance combinations refuse deterministically.
3. Zero valid assertions maps to E_EMPTY_ASSERTIONS.
4. Output ordering preserves input assertion order.
notes: Working files: src/assertions.rs, tests/refusals.rs. Assertion JSONL loader, defaults, tolerance validation, and refusal-code mapping are implemented. Focused tests passed before shared Cargo dependency churn; combined-tree validation is still running behind libduckdb-sys compilation from the candidate lane.
close_reason: Completed assertion parser, semantic validation, and unit coverage

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Starting assertion parser lane. Reserving exact file: src/assertions.rs. Keeping tests inline in that module unless scope expands.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Completed assertion parsing and semantic validation in src/assertions.rs. Added line-by-line JSONL loading, required-field checks, compare_as defaults via serde, tolerance validation for number/percent only, refusal-code mapping, and BENCH_U inline tests covering malformed JSONL, illegal tolerance, defaulting/order, empty input, and unknown compare_as. Validation: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, ubs .

title: Implement key existence, uniqueness, and null-key validation
description:
Context:
Benchmark refuses ambiguous entity lookup. This issue owns the candidate-key checks that must pass before any value comparisons begin.

Deliverables:
- key existence check against the loaded candidate relation
- uniqueness query for benchmarked rows
- null or blank key detection for benchmarked rows
- refusal detail payloads for E_KEY_NOT_FOUND, E_KEY_NOT_UNIQUE, and E_KEY_NULL

Suggested swarm lane:
lookup integrity
acceptance_criteria:
1. Scoring cannot begin if key discipline fails.
2. Missing-key, duplicate-key, and blank-key failures are distinguishable.
3. Key validation is reusable by the score engine without duplicating SQL.
notes: Taking key-check lane. Reserving only src/key_check.rs for implementation and inline tests.
close_reason: Implemented key existence, duplicate, and null/blank validation in src/key_check.rs with structured refusal details; cargo test, cargo clippy, and ubs . verified.

title: Emit benchmark tool identity and derived quality policy signals
description:
Context:
The latest benchmark plan hardened the report contract so downstream `assess v0` can match coarse benchmark quality exactly without re-deriving semantics. The implementation still omits top-level `tool` and the derived `policy_signals` surface, so reports, refusals, renderers, golden fixtures, and docs are now out of sync with the governing plan.

Deliverables:
- add top-level `tool: "benchmark"` to scoring reports and refusal envelopes
- add `policy_signals.quality_band` and `policy_signals.quality_band_basis` to scoring reports
- emit `policy_signals: {}` in refusal envelopes so downstream consumers see a stable top-level shape even on refusals
- implement the v0 mapping exactly:
  - `HIGH` when `failed = 0` and `skipped = 0`
  - `ACCEPTABLE` when `failed = 0` and `skipped > 0`
  - `LOW` when `failed > 0`
- keep raw `summary` metrics primary for ranking and auditability
- update JSON render, human render, CLI/tests, and golden fixture templates to match the new contract
- reconcile README, AGENTS, and PLAN_BENCHMARK examples with the implemented output shape

File targets:
- `src/report.rs`
- `src/refusal.rs`
- `src/render.rs`
- `src/lib.rs`
- `tests/cli.rs`
- `tests/scoring_matrix.rs`
- `tests/fixtures/expected/*.json`
- `README.md`
- `AGENTS.md`

Non-goals:
- moving proceed/block logic into `benchmark`
- replacing `summary.accuracy` or `summary.coverage` with the coarse band
- broadening `assess` into a numeric threshold engine

Suggested swarm lane:
report contract hardening
acceptance_criteria:
1. PASS, skip-only, and failed-assertion reports emit top-level `tool = "benchmark"` plus deterministic `policy_signals.quality_band` and `quality_band_basis` with the documented mapping.
2. Refusal envelopes emit top-level `tool = "benchmark"` and `policy_signals = {}` while preserving existing refusal codes and details.
3. JSON output, human output, CLI tests, render tests, and golden fixture templates all agree on the same contract without changing underlying summary math.
4. README, AGENTS, and PLAN_BENCHMARK examples are aligned with the implemented contract.
close_reason: Implemented top-level tool identity and derived quality policy signals in reports and refusals.

title: Implement lock verification before scoring
description:
Context:
Benchmark optionally accepts one or more lockfiles and must verify candidate membership and hash integrity before scoring. This issue owns the lock-check path and the structured verification result that the report layer can later render.

Deliverables:
- lockfile loading path for repeatable --lock flags
- candidate membership checks against provided lockfiles
- candidate hash verification against the matched lock entry
- structured input_verification result plus refusal wiring for lock failures

Non-goals:
- final end-to-end composition in main
- final report formatting from bd-egv

Suggested swarm lane:
input integrity / lock integration
acceptance_criteria:
1. Non-member candidates refuse with E_INPUT_NOT_LOCKED.
2. Hash drift refuses with E_INPUT_DRIFT.
3. Successful verification yields a structured result consumable by the report path.
notes:
Target files: src/lock_check.rs and lock-focused tests.
Reservation guidance: implement lock verification as a library surface that integration can call later.
close_reason: Lock verification implementation and module-local coverage are already in place in src/lock_check.rs; cargo test, cargo clippy, and ubs . now validate cleanly on the current tree.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Starting lock verification lane. Reserving exact file: src/lock_check.rs. Using module-local tests to avoid test harness collisions.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Implemented lock.v0 parsing and candidate verification in src/lock_check.rs: reads repeatable lockfiles, computes candidate sha256, matches exact member path first then unique basename fallback, returns structured input_verification, and maps refusal codes for E_IO / E_INPUT_NOT_LOCKED / E_INPUT_DRIFT. Formatting is clean; focused cargo validation is currently blocked on the unconditional DuckDB native build introduced by the candidate lane.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: Follow-up validation completed after the DuckDB/native build settled: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and ubs . are green in the shared worktree.

title: Add runtime smoke metrics and performance regression guardrails
description:
Context:
The user cares about runtime and does not want pipeline regressions. Add a dedicated performance-smoke lane that measures the integrated command path on representative fixtures and gives later agents a regression guardrail without changing benchmark semantics.

Deliverables:
- a representative large-fixture family or deterministic fixture generator for runtime checks
- a perf smoke test or bench that exercises the integrated benchmark path end to end
- phase timing capture for at least candidate load, scoring, and rendering
- a documented baseline procedure so future changes can be compared safely

Non-goals:
- changing the user-facing benchmark contract
- inventing new runtime-only features outside test or bench surfaces

Suggested swarm lane:
perf guardrails
acceptance_criteria:
1. There is a repeatable perf smoke path in the repo.
2. It measures end-to-end runtime over representative input without changing correctness semantics.
3. It captures at least candidate-load, scoring, and render timings.
4. The quality-gate issue can use it as a regression check.
notes:
Target files: tests/perf_smoke.rs or benches/runtime.rs plus perf fixtures/generators.
Reservation guidance: add timing guardrails without changing benchmark correctness semantics or normal CLI output.
close_reason: Perf generator, phase timing smoke, and baseline note added; cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and ubs . all ran clean enough for the lane.
